task_completion.description: Load the abdominal CT scan and navigate to the L3-L4 vertebral level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible with an abdominal CT scan loaded. The axial view (Red slice view) shows a cross-section of the abdomen with the vertebral body and psoas muscles visible.

task_completion.description: Identify and outline the LEFT psoas major muscle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the axial view, the psoas muscle on the anatomical left (usually the right side of the image in radiological convention) is highlighted, outlined, or covered by a segmentation mask. The label or segment name may indicate 'Left' or 'L'.

task_completion.description: Identify and outline the RIGHT psoas major muscle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the axial view, the psoas muscle on the anatomical right (usually the left side of the image in radiological convention) is highlighted, outlined, or covered by a segmentation mask. The label or segment name may indicate 'Right' or 'R'.

task_completion.description: Generate cross-sectional area measurements for the identified muscles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Segment Statistics' table, 'Markups' table, or Python console output is visible, displaying area measurements (in mm² or cm²) for the defined psoas regions.

task_completion.description: Create the JSON report file with the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal is visible showing the content of 'psoas_asymmetry_report.json'. The JSON keys 'left_psoas_area_mm2', 'right_psoas_area_mm2', 'asymmetry_index_percent', 'classification', 'smaller_side', and 'vertebral_level' are present.

task_completion.description: Verify the calculation logic and classification in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the visible JSON report, the 'asymmetry_index_percent' is correctly calculated from the two area values using the formula |L-R|/mean. The 'classification' matches the index (<10% Symmetric, 10-20% Mild, >20% Significant). The area values are within the typical physiological range (500-2600 mm²).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used 3D Slicer for the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer GUI or its internal Python console being used to view and process the medical image.

integrity.description: Ensure measurements are derived from the software, not hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in the final report match the values seen in the Slicer measurement/statistics tables in previous steps.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually segmented the psoas muscles.
integrity.visual_evidence: The segmentations or outlines shown in the screenshots correspond to the psoas muscles (paired round/oval structures lateral to the spine), not the vertebral body, kidneys, or other structures.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Typical psoas muscle cross-sectional area at L3 is 500-2600 mm². Psoas Asymmetry Index (PAI) formula: |Left - Right| / ((Left + Right) / 2) * 100. Classification thresholds: <10% is Symmetric/Normal, 10-20% is Mild Asymmetry, >20% is Significant Asymmetry. In radiological images, the patient's Left is typically on the viewer's Right.
